title: Backup Media Integrity Test Results

spl:
index=backup sourcetype=backup:integrity_test bes_scope=1 earliest=-180d
| eval test_epoch=strptime(test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-test_epoch)/86400,0)
| where match(result,"(?i)fail") OR age_days>120
| table media_id, test_date, result, age_days, vault
description: We track restore-test and checksum results for backup media containing BES configuration or security data.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes backup and sourcetypes backup:integrity_test, backup:integrity_test`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Emit HEC when integrity tests complete. (2) Define maximum age without re-test. (3) Alert storage team. (4) Document failures in CIP-008 if data loss risk. Indexes required: `index=backup`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=backup:integrity_test`, `sourcetype=backup:integrity_test``.
visualization: Table (stale or failed tests), Timeline, Single value (failed). Key fields: `age_days`, `media_id`, `result`, `test_date`, `test_epoch`, `vault`.

grandmaExplanation: We watch for we track restore-test and checksum results for backup media containing bes configuration or security data. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=backup` `sourcetype=backup:integrity_test`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit HEC when integrity tests complete. (2) Define maximum age without re-test. (3) Alert storage team. (4) Document failures in CIP-008 if data loss risk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=backup sourcetype=backup:integrity_test bes_scope=1 earliest=-180d
| eval test_epoch=strptime(test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-test_epoch)/86400,0)
| where match(result,"(?i)fail") OR age_days>120
| table media_id, test_date, result, age_days, vault
```

Understanding this SPL

**Backup Media Integrity Test Results (CIP-009-6 R2 Part 2.2)** — We track restore-test and checksum results for backup media containing BES configuration or security data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=backup` `sourcetype=backup:integrity_test`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: backup; **sourcetype**: backup:integrity_test. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=backup, sourcetype=backup:integrity_test, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **test_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(result,"(?i)fail") OR age_days>120` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_days, media_id, result, test_date, test_epoch, vault.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale or failed tests), Timeline, Single value (failed)
